content.text:
{Make straight paths for your feet.}{Hebrews 12:13}
 {It is not knowledge, but the act of learning, which grants the greatest enjoyment.}{— Carl Friedrich Gauss}

The **Gram-Schmidt process** converts any basis into an 
orthogonal basis.

content.title: Gram-Schmidt Process
content.content:
Given linearly independent $\{\vect{x}_1, \ldots, \vect{x}_p\}$, 
produce orthogonal $\{\vect{v}_1, \ldots, \vect{v}_p\}$:
$$
\vect{v}_1 &= \vect{x}_1 \\
\vect{v}_2 &= \vect{x}_2 - \proj_{\vect{v}_1}\vect{x}_2 \\
\vect{v}_3 &= \vect{x}_3 - \proj_{\vect{v}_1}\vect{x}_3 - \proj_{\vect{v}_2}\vect{x}_3 \\
&\vdots
$$
Each step subtracts projections onto previous vectors.

content.title: QR Factorization
content.content:
Any $m \times n$ matrix $A$ with independent columns can be written 
as $A = QR$ where $Q$ has orthonormal columns and $R$ is upper triangular.
content.label: Theorem

marginNotes.text:
In the wilderness prepare the way of 
the Lord; make straight in the desert a highway for our God.
